channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: EU Institute for Security Studies Traineeships 2026–2027 in Paris & Brussels | Paid EU Internship Opportunity for Graduates
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Key Details Organisation: European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS) Programme: EUISS Traineeships 2026–2027 Location: Paris, France & Brussels, Belgium Duration: 16 September 2026 – 15 July 2027 (10 months) Number of Positions: 9 traineeships Funding: Paris: EUR 1,798.96/month Brussels: EUR 1,597.95/month Application Deadline: 31 May 2026, 23:45 CEST Eligibility: EU Member State citizens […]

The post EU Institute for Security Studies Traineeships 2026–2027 in Paris & Brussels | Paid EU Internship Opportunity for Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Organisation: European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS)
  • Programme: EUISS Traineeships 2026–2027
  • Location: Paris, France & Brussels, Belgium
  • Duration: 16 September 2026 – 15 July 2027 (10 months)
  • Number of Positions: 9 traineeships
  • Funding:
    • Paris: EUR 1,798.96/month
    • Brussels: EUR 1,597.95/month
  • Application Deadline: 31 May 2026, 23:45 CEST
  • Eligibility: EU Member State citizens with a Master’s degree
  • Fields: International Relations, Political Science, Security Studies, Defence, Cyber Policy, Regional Studies, and related areas
  • Working Style: In-person with teleworking flexibility
  • Application Documents: CV, cover letter, and online application form

 

From 16 September 2026 until 15 July 2027, the European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS) seeks to recruit nine trainees to assist in its research, event organisation and outreach activities. Seven trainees will be based at EUISS Paris Headquarters, and two trainees will be based at the EUISS Brussels Liaison Office.

The EUISS traineeships provide a unique opportunity for university graduates and young professionals to build research skills, develop experience working in an EU agency, gain exposure to policy-oriented research, and deepen their understanding of EU foreign and security policy mechanisms and priorities. The EUISS traineeship promises to be an interesting and rewarding programme for career and personal development.

Throughout their EUISS traineeship, trainees work closely with a designated member of the Institute’s analyst team who will act as their mentor and supervisor. Trainees will also be asked to contribute to other analyst portfolios or to whole-of-institute projects as the need arises. Applicants are encouraged to explore analyst profiles on the EUISS website and to think about which areas they would like to assist in addition to the selected portfolio.

For the 2026-2027 traineeship programme, one trainee position is available in each of the following domains:

  • Asia/Global China;
  • Cyber and Digital Technologies;
  • European Defence Policy (Brussels Liaison Office);
  • Indo-Pacific (Brussels Liaison Office);
  • Middle East and North Africa;
  • Russia and the Eastern Neighbourhood;
  • Sub-Saharan Africa;
  • Transnational Security (the concept includes among others, addressing hybrid threats, disinformation, election meddling, covert military and other action, and resilience building);
  • EUISS Director’s office (assisting the Director and his team in various research, coordination and outreach tasks).

When submitting their applications, candidates must select one single domain of expertise/profile.

The duties and responsibilities of trainees will include:

  • Desk and library research on topics selected by their supervisor;
  • Preparation of research dossiers, write-up of material, completing/updating of the mapping of research topics;
  • Assistance in the organisation of events, including performance of administrative tasks, logistical support, finding and contacting speakers, preparing conference agendas and material, taking notes etc.;
  • Updating of EUISS databases and mapping contacts;
  • Other related duties as required.

Eligibility criteria and required competencies

– Nationality:

Candidates must be citizens of an EU member state.

– Education:

Candidates must possess a Master’s degree in political science, international relations, economics, or a related field by the time the traineeship starts.

– Language skills:

Candidates must be fluent in English and have a thorough knowledge of at least one other EU language. Additional knowledge of one or more languages of the region of expertise will be considered an asset.

– Core competencies:

  • Experience in data gathering and research;
  • Experience in event organisation;
  • Computer literacy;
  • Previous relevant field work and/or research experience in the selected portfolio will be considered an asset;
  • Proven analytical and report writing skills will be considered an asset.

–  Behavioural competencies:

  • Highly organised and self-motivated;
  • Creativity, flexibility and ability to work to deadlines;
  • Maturity, initiative, tact and high sense of responsibility;
  • Demonstrated ability to work in a multinational team.

Conditions

– Duration:

The traineeship will be for a period of 10 months, running from 16 September 2026 until 15 July 2027 (at the moment of sending the offer to selected candidates, the EUISS reserves the right to adjust the starting date).

– Location:

Seven trainees will be based at EUISS headquarters in Paris and two at the EUISS Brussels Liaison Office.

– Traineeship grant:

  • Paris-based trainees will be awarded a grant of EUR 1,798.96 per calendar month.
  • Brussels-based trainees will be awarded a grant of EUR 1,597.95 per calendar month.

– Other conditions:

Trainees are bound by the same working time arrangements as EUISS staff members. Trainees equally benefit of the teleworking policy that is implemented at the EUISS (important note: except for very limited periods, teleworking is not allowed from countries outside France or Belgium).

Trainees are entitled to 1.5 day’s leave per month, as well as statutory holidays.

Health and accident insurance is compulsory. The EUISS does not provide such insurance. Proof of insurance must be presented to the EUISS at the latest on the first day of the traineeship and should be valid for the entire period of the traineeship.

The EUISS cannot pay for travel expenses or accommodation, nor can it assist trainees in finding accommodation in Paris or Brussels beyond providing them with general information and advice.

The deadline for applications is 23h59 on Sunday 31 May. Applications received after this deadline will not be considered.

Instructions for applicants:

  • Complete the online application form;
  • Attach a detailed CV, maximum 2 pages, in English (in .pdf);
  • Attach a cover letter, maximum 1 page, in English (in .pdf) addressed to the EUISS Director, outlining the reasons for the interest in the Institute’s traineeship programme and the applicant’s main strengths.
  • Select candidates may be requested to undertake a written assignment.

All applicants will be informed of the results of their application by the end of the procedure.

Click here to download the rules governing the traineeship programme at the EUISS.

Find out more here 

The processing of personal data by the EUISS is governed by Regulation (EU) 2018/1725 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 October 2018 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data by the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Regulation (EC) N° 45/2001 and Decision N° 1247/2002/EC. By submitting an application for a traineeship, the applicant consents to the processing of the personal data contained in the application form and the supporting documents enclosed therewith, in the sense of Article 5 (a) of the Regulation. The personal data in question is collected by the EUISS for the sole purpose of the selection of trainees.

 

Subscribe to our newsletter

We will keep you informed about our latest publication

The post EU Institute for Security Studies Traineeships 2026–2027 in Paris & Brussels | Paid EU Internship Opportunity for Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded University of Manchester PhD 2026 in Net Zero Decision Making and Climate Policy Research (UK Students Only)
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The University of Manchester is now accepting applications for a fully funded PhD project titled “Institutional Cultures and Citizen Engagement for Net Zero Decision Making” under the prestigious PACT (Production and Consumption Transformations) Centre. This exciting doctoral opportunity is designed for students interested in climate governance, public policy, citizen participation, institutional decision-making, political economy, and […]

The post Fully Funded University of Manchester PhD 2026 in Net Zero Decision Making and Climate Policy Research (UK Students Only) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Manchester is now accepting applications for a fully funded PhD project titled “Institutional Cultures and Citizen Engagement for Net Zero Decision Making” under the prestigious PACT (Production and Consumption Transformations) Centre. This exciting doctoral opportunity is designed for students interested in climate governance, public policy, citizen participation, institutional decision-making, political economy, and net-zero transitions in the United Kingdom.

The project offers successful applicants the opportunity to conduct cutting-edge interdisciplinary research on climate policy and societal transformation while joining one of the UK’s leading climate research environments.

The fully funded PhD studentship will begin in October 2026 and provides financial support through tuition coverage and an annual tax-free stipend of £21,805 for the 2026/27 academic year.

About the PACT Centre

The PhD is part of the newly established PACT Centre, a four-year research initiative jointly funded by:

  • UK Research and Innovation (UKRI)
  • UK Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ)

PACT is a collaborative partnership between:

  • The University of Manchester
  • The University of Leeds
  • The University of Sussex

The Centre focuses on understanding how production and consumption systems can be transformed to achieve climate goals, energy efficiency, and sustainable societal transitions.

Its work aims to produce actionable insights that influence:

  • Government policy
  • Climate governance
  • Public engagement strategies
  • Sustainable economic systems
  • Net-zero transition planning

PACT collaborates closely with:

  • Government departments
  • Civil society organizations
  • Campaign groups
  • Private sector stakeholders

The research programme investigates how political, economic, and societal systems shape the UK’s transition toward net zero emissions while ensuring fairness, inclusivity, and effectiveness in policy implementation.

Focus Areas of the PACT Research Programme

PACT organizes its research around three major analytical lenses:

1. Accounting and Modelling

This area focuses on:

  • Quantifying impacts across production and consumption systems
  • Resource efficiency analysis
  • Climate modelling
  • Evaluation of sustainability interventions

2. Decision-Making Institutions and Cultures

This research stream examines:

  • Institutional cultures
  • Policy development processes
  • Governance systems
  • Coalitions of influence
  • Economic interest groups
  • Political decision-making structures

3. Public Impact and Engagement

This lens investigates:

  • Citizen participation in climate policymaking
  • Public engagement mechanisms
  • Policy impacts on communities
  • Social inclusion in environmental governance

About the Research Team

At the University of Manchester, the PACT team includes:

  • Prof Rebecca Willis
  • Dr Andy Yuille
  • Mathilde Rainard

The successful PhD candidate will join the highly respected Climate Citizens research group, which includes around 15 research staff and doctoral researchers within:

  • The Tyndall Centre
  • The Politics Department at the University of Manchester

This provides a strong interdisciplinary environment for climate governance and public policy research.

Research Areas for the PhD Proposal

Applicants are expected to submit a research proposal aligned with the aims of PACT. The University encourages candidates to shape their proposal according to their own interests and expertise while considering the supervisors’ research areas.

The programme is especially interested in proposals covering:

Potential Research Topics

  1. Economic Interest Groups and Climate Policymaking
    • How economic actors influence climate policy
    • Institutional resistance to net-zero transitions
    • Policy disruption and lobbying practices
  2. Cross-National Comparisons
    • Comparative studies on public participation in climate governance
    • Citizen engagement models across countries
  3. Public Engagement and Policymaking
    • How citizen input influences government decisions
    • Translating public consultations into policy outcomes
  4. Climate Governance Case Studies
    • Formal and informal routes of influence
    • Trade unions and citizen lobby groups
    • Public consultation mechanisms
  5. Cars and Homes as Net-Zero Case Studies
    • Sustainable transport policy
    • Housing decarbonization
    • Consumer behaviour and climate transitions
  6. Innovative Research Methods
    • Qualitative research
    • Mixed-methods approaches
    • Process tracing methodologies
    • Existing dataset analysis

Applicants may also use datasets already available within the project, including:

  • Two-year citizens’ panel datasets
  • Policymaker discussion group datasets

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for this fully funded PhD opportunity, applicants should:

  • Hold or expect to obtain at least a 2:1 honours degree or a Master’s degree (or international equivalent)
  • Have academic training in a relevant discipline such as:
    • Political science
    • Sociology
    • Political economy
    • Geography
    • Public policy
    • Organizational psychology
    • Climate governance
    • Social sciences

Desirable Qualifications

The University also values:

  • Professional experience in climate-related fields
  • Experience in public engagement research
  • Interest in sustainability and net-zero policy
  • Qualitative or mixed-methods research expertise

Funding Benefits

This is a fully funded 3.5-year PhD project.

The successful applicant will receive:

  • Full tuition fee coverage
  • Annual tax-free stipend of £21,805 for 2026/27
  • Expected annual stipend increases
  • Access to world-class research facilities
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Membership within the Climate Citizens research group

The programme also supports:

  • Flexible study arrangements
  • Inclusive research environments
  • Diversity and equality initiatives

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents:

  1. Research Proposal (maximum two pages)
  2. CV
  3. Academic transcripts and certificates
  4. Supporting statement outlining:
    • Research motivation
    • Relevant experience
    • Skills and techniques
    • Previous research findings
  5. Two referee contacts
  6. English language certificate (if applicable)

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Apply Here

Important Deadline and Start Date

  • Application Deadline: Tuesday, 9 June 2026
  • Programme Start Date: October 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early because the advert may close before the official deadline.

Why This PhD Opportunity Matters

This PhD represents a major opportunity for researchers passionate about:

  • Climate governance
  • Public policy
  • Citizen participation
  • Institutional change
  • Sustainable development
  • Net-zero transitions

The project combines rigorous academic research with real-world policy relevance, allowing scholars to contribute directly to national and international climate transformation strategies.

With increasing global attention on climate action and public engagement, this programme positions successful candidates at the forefront of sustainability and governance research.

About the University of Manchester

The University of Manchester is one of the UK’s leading research-intensive universities and is internationally recognized for excellence in climate science, public policy, and interdisciplinary sustainability research.

The university actively promotes:

  • Equality and diversity
  • Inclusive research cultures
  • Flexible study arrangements
  • Support for applicants from diverse backgrounds

Its strong global reputation and extensive research networks make it an ideal environment for doctoral study in climate governance and public engagement.

For more information about this PhD scholarship visit here

For more global scholarships on OFY, visit here

The post Fully Funded University of Manchester PhD 2026 in Net Zero Decision Making and Climate Policy Research (UK Students Only) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid Warsaw Security Forum 2026 Internship Program in Poland Opens for International Students and Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Organizing Committee of the Warsaw Security Forum (WSF) 2026 has officially opened applications for its highly competitive paid internship programme, offering students and young professionals an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience in international diplomacy, security policy, event management, communications, and global affairs. The internship programme will run from mid-June to mid-October 2026 and […]

The post Paid Warsaw Security Forum 2026 Internship Program in Poland Opens for International Students and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organizing Committee of the Warsaw Security Forum (WSF) 2026 has officially opened applications for its highly competitive paid internship programme, offering students and young professionals an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience in international diplomacy, security policy, event management, communications, and global affairs.

The internship programme will run from mid-June to mid-October 2026 and provides selected interns with direct exposure to one of Europe’s most influential international security forums.

Participants will work closely with experts, diplomats, policymakers, and global stakeholders involved in organizing the Warsaw Security Forum 2026, widely recognized as one of Central and Eastern Europe’s leading security and foreign policy events.

About the Warsaw Security Forum (WSF)

The Warsaw Security Forum is an internationally recognized platform that convenes:

  • Government officials
  • Diplomats
  • Security experts
  • Military leaders
  • International organizations
  • Think tanks
  • Business leaders
  • Civil society representatives

The forum addresses critical issues related to:

  • International security
  • Defense cooperation
  • NATO and transatlantic relations
  • Geopolitics
  • Cybersecurity
  • European security policy
  • Democratic resilience

The event is organized by the Casimir Pulaski Foundation in collaboration with leading international partners and institutions.

Through this internship programme, participants will gain behind-the-scenes experience in organizing a world-class diplomatic and security event while building valuable professional networks in international affairs and policy circles.

Internship Duration

The internship programme will take place from:

  • Mid-June 2026 to Mid-October 2026

This multi-month placement provides interns with sufficient time to gain practical experience and actively contribute to the preparation and execution of the Warsaw Security Forum 2026.

Benefits of the Warsaw Security Forum Internship Program

Selected interns will receive numerous professional and career development benefits, including:

1. Paid Internship Experience

The programme is a paid internship opportunity, allowing participants to gain professional experience while receiving financial support.

2. Networking Opportunities

Interns will:

  • Build professional relationships with international experts
  • Interact with diplomats and policymakers
  • Connect with professionals in security and international affairs
  • Expand their global professional network

3. Direct Participation in WSF 2026

Interns will contribute to organizing one of Europe’s premier security forums and may attend:

  • High-level panels
  • International discussions
  • Diplomatic sessions
  • Security and policy events

4. Access to Additional Events

Participants may also attend selected events organized by:

  • The Casimir Pulaski Foundation
  • The European Academy of Diplomacy

5. Official Internship Certificate

All interns will receive an official certificate upon successful completion of the programme to strengthen their professional portfolio and CV.

6. Career Advancement Opportunities

Outstanding interns may receive:

  • Letters of recommendation
  • Opportunities for future collaboration
  • Potential full-time employment offers with the Casimir Pulaski Foundation

Available Internship Positions

The Warsaw Security Forum 2026 internship programme offers opportunities in several departments.

Internship Roles Include:

  1. Assistant to the Head of the Organizing Committee
  2. VIP Liaison & Delegations Support
  3. Business Partners Support
  4. Participants & Registration Support
  5. Logistics Support
  6. Communication & Media Support

These roles provide practical experience across multiple areas of international event coordination, communications, administration, and diplomacy.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants interested in joining the programme should meet the following criteria:

Academic Requirements

Candidates should have completed at least one year of university studies in fields such as:

  • International Relations
  • Law
  • European Studies
  • Economics
  • Information Technology (IT)
  • Graphic Design

Applicants from related disciplines with strong interest in international affairs may also be considered.

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English
  • Knowledge of Polish is considered an advantage but is not mandatory

Technical and Professional Skills

Successful candidates should possess:

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Strong organizational skills
  • Excellent interpersonal communication abilities
  • Attention to detail
  • Ability to work independently
  • Passion for international affairs, diplomacy, and defense

Why This Internship Matters

The Warsaw Security Forum internship programme offers a rare opportunity for students and young professionals to gain direct experience in:

  • International diplomacy
  • Global security policy
  • International event management
  • Strategic communications
  • Policy coordination
  • International cooperation

Participants will not only contribute to a globally recognized event but also strengthen their professional profiles for future careers in:

  • International organizations
  • Foreign affairs
  • Think tanks
  • Government institutions
  • NGOs
  • Policy research
  • Global communications

How to Apply

Interested candidates can learn more about the programme and submit their applications through the official Warsaw Security Forum internship portal.

Apply Here

For inquiries, applicants may contact:

  • wsf@pulaski.pl

Applicants are advised to include the subject of their inquiry in the email title.

Application Timeline

  • Internship Period: Mid-June – Mid-October 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the programme.

About the Casimir Pulaski Foundation

The Casimir Pulaski Foundation is a leading independent think tank specializing in foreign policy, international security, defense, and transatlantic relations.

The Foundation works closely with international experts and institutions to promote democratic values, strategic dialogue, and international cooperation.

For more information about this internship visit here

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, visit here

The post Paid Warsaw Security Forum 2026 Internship Program in Poland Opens for International Students and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Opportunity in New Delhi, India 2026 | Apply for the UNV Research Role with UNDP Before 22 May 2026
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has announced an exciting UN Volunteer opportunity for aspiring researchers and development professionals through its Associate (Research) position based in New Delhi, India. This opportunity provides candidates with a chance to contribute to impactful international development work while gaining valuable experience within one of the world’s leading development organizations. […]

The post Opportunity in New Delhi, India 2026 | Apply for the UNV Research Role with UNDP Before 22 May 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has announced an exciting UN Volunteer opportunity for aspiring researchers and development professionals through its Associate (Research) position based in New Delhi, India. This opportunity provides candidates with a chance to contribute to impactful international development work while gaining valuable experience within one of the world’s leading development organizations.

The UN Volunteer (UNV) programme continues to create pathways for talented individuals who are passionate about sustainable development, research, public policy, innovation, social impact, and global cooperation. The Associate (Research) opportunity with UNDP in New Delhi is ideal for candidates seeking hands-on experience in international development research, policy analysis, programme support, and evidence-based development solutions.

Applications for this opportunity close on 22 May 2026.

About the UNDP Associate (Research) UN Volunteer Opportunity

The Associate (Research) role is hosted by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in New Delhi, India, under the United Nations Volunteers (UNV) programme. This position offers individuals the opportunity to support development-focused research initiatives and contribute to projects that align with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

UNDP is globally recognized for its work in reducing poverty, supporting democratic governance, advancing climate action, promoting gender equality, and fostering sustainable economic development. Through this volunteer opportunity, selected candidates will gain practical exposure to research-driven development interventions and international programme implementation.

The position is based in New Delhi, one of India’s major hubs for policy dialogue, innovation, international development collaboration, and public sector engagement.

Why This UN Volunteer Opportunity Matters

The UN Volunteer Associate (Research) role presents an excellent platform for young professionals, graduates, researchers, and development enthusiasts who want to:

  • Gain experience working with the United Nations system.
  • Contribute to evidence-based policy and development initiatives.
  • Develop research and analytical skills in an international environment.
  • Build networks with development practitioners, policymakers, and researchers.
  • Strengthen professional experience in sustainable development.
  • Enhance future career opportunities within the UN system and international organizations.

For candidates passionate about development research and global impact, this opportunity can become a stepping stone toward long-term international careers.

Key Highlights of the UNDP Associate (Research) Opportunity

Position Details

  • Position Title: Associate (Research)
  • Organization: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • Programme: United Nations Volunteers (UNV)
  • Duty Station: New Delhi, India
  • Application Deadline: 22 May 2026
  • Opportunity Type: UN Volunteer Assignment

Responsibilities of the Associate (Research)

Although detailed terms of reference may be available on the official application portal, research-focused UN Volunteer positions generally involve:

  1. Conducting background research and policy analysis.
  2. Supporting data collection and interpretation.
  3. Assisting with development reports and documentation.
  4. Preparing presentations, briefs, and analytical summaries.
  5. Supporting programme implementation and monitoring.
  6. Contributing to knowledge management initiatives.
  7. Collaborating with internal teams and external stakeholders.
  8. Assisting with sustainable development and policy projects.

Candidates with strong analytical thinking, writing abilities, and research experience may find this role highly beneficial.

Benefits of the UNDP UN Volunteer Programme

Participating in a UN Volunteer assignment offers both professional and personal growth opportunities. Benefits may include:

Professional Development

  • Exposure to international development operations.
  • Experience working within the United Nations system.
  • Development of policy and research expertise.
  • Enhanced communication and analytical skills.
  • Networking opportunities with global experts and practitioners.

International Experience

  • Opportunity to work in a multicultural environment.
  • Collaboration with professionals from diverse backgrounds.
  • Exposure to development challenges and innovative solutions.

Career Advancement

  • Valuable experience for future UN and international organization applications.
  • Improved competitiveness in global development careers.
  • Opportunities to build a strong professional portfolio.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity may be suitable for:

  • Young professionals interested in international development.
  • Researchers and policy analysts.
  • Graduates in social sciences, economics, development studies, public policy, international relations, statistics, or related disciplines.
  • Individuals passionate about sustainable development and global impact.
  • Candidates interested in UN careers and volunteer opportunities.

Applicants with previous research, analytical, academic, or development-related experience may have an added advantage.

How to Apply for the UNDP Associate (Research) Opportunity

Interested candidates are required to create a profile on the UN Volunteer Unified Volunteer Platform before applying.

Application Steps

  1. Create a profile on the UN Volunteer platform.
  2. Complete your profile with educational background, professional experience, and skills.
  3. Apply for the Associate (Research) opportunity before the deadline.
  4. Ensure all required information is correctly submitted.

Apply Here 

Important Deadline

The application deadline for the UNDP Associate (Research) UN Volunteer opportunity is:

22 May 2026

Candidates are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Final Thoughts

The UNDP Associate (Research) UN Volunteer opportunity in New Delhi, India represents a valuable pathway for motivated individuals seeking to contribute to sustainable development and gain professional international experience.

With the global demand for research-driven policy solutions increasing, opportunities like this help emerging professionals build relevant skills while supporting impactful initiatives.

Applicants interested in international development, policy research, and United Nations careers should take advantage of this opportunity before the 22 May 2026 deadline.

For more information on this volunteering opportunity visit here 

For more global volunteer programmes on OFY, visit here

The post Opportunity in New Delhi, India 2026 | Apply for the UNV Research Role with UNDP Before 22 May 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ACU Expert Group Fellowships and Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers and Universities Across the Commonwealth
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU) has officially extended the application deadline for its ACU Expert Group Fellowships and ACU Expert Group Grants 2026, providing researchers, academics, and universities across the Commonwealth with more time to apply for these prestigious funding opportunities. The new deadline for both opportunities is: 9 June 2026 at 3:00 PM […]

The post ACU Expert Group Fellowships and Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers and Universities Across the Commonwealth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU) has officially extended the application deadline for its ACU Expert Group Fellowships and ACU Expert Group Grants 2026, providing researchers, academics, and universities across the Commonwealth with more time to apply for these prestigious funding opportunities.

The new deadline for both opportunities is:

9 June 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

These programmes support impactful research and university-led initiatives focused on some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including:

  • Health
  • Oceans
  • Sustainable Urbanisation

Although both programmes are connected to the ACU Expert Groups, they target different types of applicants and offer different funding structures and opportunities.

Below is a detailed breakdown of each opportunity.

1. ACU Expert Group Fellowships 2026

The ACU Expert Group Fellowships 2026 are designed to support researchers conducting policy-relevant research on major global issues affecting Commonwealth countries and the wider international community.

This fellowship programme offers researchers the opportunity to contribute directly to international policy discussions while receiving financial support for their work.

Fellowship Research Themes

For the 2026 cycle, the fellowship programme focuses on:

  • Health
  • Oceans

Applicants are expected to submit research proposals that address current challenges, policy gaps, innovative solutions, or evidence-based recommendations within these thematic areas.

Fellowship Funding

Successful applicants can receive:

  • Up to GBP 13,000 per fellowship

The funding is intended to support research activities, project implementation, analysis, dissemination, and production of policy-focused outputs.

Major Benefits of the Fellowship

1. Research Funding

Researchers receive substantial funding support to conduct high-impact policy-relevant research.

2. International Recognition

Selected fellows gain visibility within international academic and policy communities.

3. Contribution to High-Level Policy Discussions

Research findings from the fellowship programme will help inform discussions at major Commonwealth forums.

4. Opportunity to Present at CHOGM 2026

One of the most exciting benefits is the possibility of presenting research at the 2026 Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM) in Antigua and Barbuda.

This provides fellows with direct exposure to policymakers, global leaders, and international stakeholders.

5. Global Networking Opportunities

Fellows become part of an international network of experts, academics, and policymakers across Commonwealth countries.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship opportunity is ideal for:

  • Researchers
  • Academics
  • Policy analysts
  • University scholars
  • Experts in Health or Oceans-related fields
  • Early-career and mid-career researchers

Applicants with strong policy-oriented research ideas are strongly encouraged to apply.

Click here for more and to apply 

For more global fellowship opportunities Click here

2. ACU Expert Group Grants 2026

The ACU Expert Group Grants 2026 are designed to support collaborative university projects and institutional initiatives linked to the ACU Expert Groups.

Unlike the fellowships, which focus on individual policy research, the grants support practical university-based activities and collaborations.

Grant Themes

The grants support projects connected to:

  • Health
  • Oceans
  • Sustainable Urbanisation

Projects may include:

  • Workshops
  • Research collaborations
  • Knowledge-sharing activities
  • Institutional partnerships
  • Academic events
  • Capacity-building initiatives
  • Collaborative projects among universities

Grant Funding

For each Expert Group:

  • Three grants of up to GBP 4,000 each will be awarded

The grants are intended to support smaller-scale but impactful academic and institutional initiatives.

Benefits of the Grant Programme

1. Support for University Projects

The grants provide financial support for collaborative academic activities and institutional initiatives.

2. Strengthening Commonwealth Collaboration

The programme promotes partnerships and knowledge exchange among universities across Commonwealth countries.

3. Encouraging Innovation

The grants help institutions develop creative and interdisciplinary approaches to global challenges.

4. Expanding Institutional Networks

Participating universities gain opportunities to connect with international academic and policy communities.

Who Should Apply?

The grant programme is suitable for:

  • Universities
  • Academic departments
  • Research groups
  • Institutional partnerships
  • Collaborative academic teams
  • University-led projects

Applicants should demonstrate how their project aligns with the selected thematic area and contributes to collaboration or impact.

Click here for more and to apply.

For more global grant opportunities Click here

Why These Opportunities Matter

The ACU Expert Group programmes play an important role in promoting evidence-based solutions to global challenges affecting Commonwealth nations.

Through research funding, academic collaboration, and international networking, these opportunities help researchers and universities contribute meaningfully to policy development and sustainable progress.

The programmes also strengthen international cooperation among Commonwealth institutions while supporting innovation, research excellence, and interdisciplinary engagement.

Application Deadline

The deadline for both opportunities is:

9 June 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals early and ensure that all required documents are submitted before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The ACU Expert Group Fellowships and Grants 2026 provide exceptional opportunities for researchers and universities seeking funding, international collaboration, and policy impact.

Whether through high-level policy research or collaborative university initiatives, these programmes offer valuable support for addressing major global development challenges.

Researchers and institutions working in Health, Oceans, and Sustainable Urbanisation should strongly consider applying before the extended deadline.

 

The post ACU Expert Group Fellowships and Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers and Universities Across the Commonwealth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026: Major International Expansion Opportunity for African Startups Entering Global Markets
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The African Continental Free Trade Area Secretariat, in partnership with the Korea Africa Foundation, has officially launched applications for the AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026, a major continental initiative designed to support high-potential African startups seeking international growth, market expansion, and strategic business partnerships. The programme aims to strengthen Africa’s innovation ecosystem by […]

The post AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026: Major International Expansion Opportunity for African Startups Entering Global Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Continental Free Trade Area Secretariat, in partnership with the Korea Africa Foundation, has officially launched applications for the AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026, a major continental initiative designed to support high-potential African startups seeking international growth, market expansion, and strategic business partnerships.

The programme aims to strengthen Africa’s innovation ecosystem by helping startups improve competitiveness, expand across borders, attract investment opportunities, and access international markets, particularly in the Republic of Korea and other global economies.

As Africa’s startup ecosystem continues to grow rapidly, initiatives like this play an important role in connecting African entrepreneurs with global business networks, commercialization opportunities, mentorship support, and international partnerships that can accelerate long-term business success.

The programme aligns closely with the broader objectives of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), which seeks to strengthen intra-African trade, industrial development, innovation, and private-sector-led economic transformation across the continent.

About AfCFTA

The African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) is one of the world’s largest free trade areas, focused on:

  • Promoting intra-African trade
  • Accelerating industrialization
  • Strengthening economic integration
  • Supporting private sector growth
  • Enhancing competitiveness across Africa

AfCFTA aims to create a stronger and more interconnected African economy capable of competing globally.

The AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program

This program is a flagship startup support initiative focused on:

  • Startup acceleration
  • International market expansion
  • Cross-border partnerships
  • Investment facilitation
  • Commercialization of African innovation
  • Strengthening Africa-Korea business cooperation

The programme specifically supports startups that demonstrate:

  • High growth potential
  • Scalable business models
  • Innovative solutions
  • Readiness for international business engagement

The initiative reflects growing efforts to position African startups as globally competitive enterprises capable of expanding beyond domestic markets into regional and international ecosystems.

Main Objectives of the Program

The programme seeks to support African startups through several strategic goals.

1. Strengthening Startup Competitiveness

The initiative aims to help startups:

  • Improve operational capacity
  • Enhance scalability
  • Build sustainable business structures
  • Increase market competitiveness

2. Supporting International Expansion

Participating startups receive support designed to:

  • Facilitate entry into Korean markets
  • Explore global expansion opportunities
  • Develop international business readiness
  • Build export-oriented business strategies

3. Facilitating Strategic Partnerships

The programme promotes:

  • Africa-Korea business collaboration
  • Investor engagement
  • Cross-border partnerships
  • Commercial cooperation opportunities

4. Promoting Innovation and Commercialization

The programme seeks to:

  • Accelerate innovative African solutions
  • Support commercialization pathways
  • Encourage technology adoption
  • Advance industrial and digital transformation

Why This Programme Matters

African startups continue to face challenges such as:

  • Limited access to international markets
  • Insufficient funding opportunities
  • Weak commercialization pathways
  • Limited global business exposure
  • Challenges scaling across borders

The AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program addresses these barriers by connecting startups to:

  • Global business ecosystems
  • International partnerships
  • Strategic networks
  • Investment opportunities
  • Market expansion support

The programme also contributes to AfCFTA’s vision of building:

  • A unified African market
  • Increased intra-African trade
  • Industrial growth
  • Stronger regional integration

Priority Sectors

Eligible startups must operate within sectors aligned with AfCFTA priorities.

1. Fintech

This includes startups focused on:

  • Digital payments
  • Financial inclusion
  • Mobile banking
  • Blockchain solutions
  • Financial technology innovation

2. E-commerce

Businesses supporting:

  • Digital marketplaces
  • Online retail
  • Cross-border commerce
  • Digital trade platforms

3. Logistics

Startups focused on:

  • Supply chain innovation
  • Transportation technology
  • Trade facilitation
  • Delivery systems
  • Logistics optimization

4. Agri-tech

Agricultural technology ventures working on:

  • Smart farming
  • Food systems innovation
  • Agricultural productivity
  • Agribusiness technologies

5. Manufacturing

Startups contributing to:

  • Industrial production
  • Local manufacturing
  • Value addition
  • Production technologies

6. Digital Platforms

Technology-driven businesses offering:

  • Digital services
  • Platform-based solutions
  • Software innovation
  • Technology infrastructure

Benefits of the Program

Selected startups will receive access to multiple growth and expansion opportunities.

1. Business Development Support

Participants may receive:

  • Strategic advisory support
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Business acceleration support
  • Growth readiness assistance

2. International Market Access

The programme supports:

  • Entry into Korean markets
  • Exposure to global business ecosystems
  • International trade opportunities
  • Cross-border business development

3. Networking Opportunities

Participants gain opportunities to connect with:

  • Investors
  • Business leaders
  • Strategic partners
  • International stakeholders
  • Innovation ecosystems

4. Partnership and Investment Opportunities

The programme facilitates:

  • Strategic collaborations
  • Potential investment engagement
  • Partnership development
  • Business matchmaking

5. Enhanced Global Visibility

Selected startups gain:

  • Increased exposure
  • International recognition
  • Continental business positioning

Who Should Apply?

This programme is ideal for:

  • African tech founders
  • Startup entrepreneurs
  • Innovation-driven SMEs
  • Growth-stage startups
  • Businesses seeking international expansion
  • Export-oriented ventures
  • African digital innovators

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, startups must:

  • Be based in Africa
  • Have innovative and scalable business models
  • Demonstrate readiness for international expansion
  • Show interest in entering Korean and global markets
  • Operate within AfCFTA priority sectors

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

1. Completed Application Form

Applicants must carefully complete the official programme application form.

2. Business Plan

The business plan should clearly outline:

  • Business model
  • Market strategy
  • Growth potential
  • Operational structure
  • Financial sustainability

3. Supporting Documents

Applicants must provide all supporting documentation required under the programme guidelines.

4. Startup Partnership Plan

This document should explain:

  • Partnership goals
  • Collaboration interests
  • International engagement strategy

5. Program Expenditure Plan

Applicants are expected to provide:

  • Budget allocation plans
  • Resource utilization strategy
  • Financial planning details

Application Deadline

24 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare all required documents in advance.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email to:

SME.Support@au-afcfta.org

Applicants should ensure:

  • All documents are complete
  • Files are properly organized
  • Information is accurate and professionally presented

Startup Partnership Plan

Program Expenditure Plan

Why African Startups Should Not Miss This Opportunity

This programme offers more than traditional startup training. It provides:

  • International market exposure
  • Strategic business partnerships
  • Cross-border collaboration
  • Access to Korean innovation ecosystems
  • Opportunities for scaling globally

For African startups seeking international growth, the programme can serve as a major gateway to global expansion.

Click HERE to learn more about the AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026.

Discover more global Acceleration programs on OFY.

The post AfCFTA Startup Acceleration & Partnership Program 2026: Major International Expansion Opportunity for African Startups Entering Global Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Florence PhD Programmes 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunities with Scholarships, International Support, and Research Funding
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The University of Florence has officially opened applications for its prestigious XLII Cycle PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. Hosted in one of Italy’s most renowned academic institutions, these doctoral programmes provide exceptional opportunities for students and researchers from around the world to pursue advanced research across multiple disciplines while benefiting from scholarships, international […]

The post University of Florence PhD Programmes 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunities with Scholarships, International Support, and Research Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Florence has officially opened applications for its prestigious XLII Cycle PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. Hosted in one of Italy’s most renowned academic institutions, these doctoral programmes provide exceptional opportunities for students and researchers from around the world to pursue advanced research across multiple disciplines while benefiting from scholarships, international mobility support, and access to a vibrant academic environment.

The call for applications includes several doctoral programmes headquartered at the University of Florence, with positions available both with and without scholarships. The programmes are scheduled to begin on November 1, 2026, and applications must be submitted before the official deadline of June 11, 2026.

For international students, especially those interested in pursuing doctoral studies in Europe, this is an outstanding opportunity to study in Italy while engaging in world-class research and academic collaboration.

About the University of Florence PhD Programmes

The University of Florence is one of Italy’s oldest and most respected universities, known for its strong research culture, international collaborations, and commitment to academic excellence.

The XLII Cycle PhD Programmes are designed to:

  • Develop advanced research skills
  • Promote scientific innovation
  • Encourage international academic cooperation
  • Prepare researchers for academic and professional careers
  • Foster multidisciplinary and multicultural research environments

These doctoral programmes are governed by the Italian Ministry of University and Research (MUR) and are subject to accreditation procedures in accordance with Ministerial Decree no. 226/2021.

Duration of the PhD Programmes

The duration of the doctoral programmes is:

  • 3 years full-time

Planned start date:

  • November 1, 2026

Students admitted to the programmes are expected to undertake continuous research and academic activities throughout the doctoral cycle.

Fields and Research Areas

The call covers multiple PhD programmes hosted by the University of Florence. The specific research areas, available scholarships, reserved positions, and admission procedures are outlined in Annex 2 of the official call.

Depending on the programme selected, candidates may engage in research areas such as:

  • Engineering
  • Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Social sciences
  • Technology
  • Health sciences
  • Environmental studies
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Cybersecurity
  • Space research
  • International studies
  • Economics
  • Arts and culture

Some scholarships are linked to specific research topics and funded by external organizations or public institutions.

Number of Available Positions and Scholarships

The University of Florence offers:

  • Multiple doctoral positions
  • Scholarship-funded positions
  • Positions without scholarships
  • Reserved positions for specific categories
  • Additional positions that may become available later

The number of available positions may increase if additional scholarship funding becomes available before enrolment.

Some scholarships are funded by:

  • Public institutions
  • Private organizations
  • Italian Space Agency (ASI)
  • National Cybersecurity Agency (ACN)
  • International research entities

Scholarship Benefits

The PhD scholarships offered by the University of Florence provide substantial financial support to selected candidates.

Annual Scholarship Amount

The annual scholarship amount is:

  • €16,243 per year (before social security charges)

The scholarship is paid monthly in arrears.

Additional Financial Benefits

In addition to the annual scholarship, PhD students may receive several other financial advantages.

1. Research Abroad Funding Increase

Students conducting research activities abroad can receive:

  • A 50% increase in scholarship funding

This support is available for:

  • Up to 12 months abroad
  • Up to 18 months for co-supervised international doctoral programmes

2. Research Budget

Each doctoral student receives:

  • A research budget equal to at least 10% of the scholarship amount

This funding supports:

  • Research activities
  • Academic travel
  • Conference participation
  • Research materials
  • Specialized training

3. One-Off Financial Support for International Students

To encourage international participation, the University of Florence, supported by the Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Firenze, offers:

  • One-off financial support of up to €5,000

This support targets international doctoral students meeting specific criteria.

Who Can Receive the €5,000 International Support?

The one-off grant is intended for candidates who:

  • Reside in a non-EU country
  • Hold a foreign qualification for PhD access
  • Are under 35 years old

Importantly:

  • No separate application is required
  • Beneficiaries are selected automatically
  • The grant may be combined with scholarships

If funds remain available, the support may also extend to:

  • EU residents studying abroad
  • Candidates with degrees obtained outside Tuscany

Eligibility Criteria

The University of Florence welcomes applicants from all nationalities without age or citizenship restrictions for the main PhD competition.

Eligible applicants include candidates holding:

  • A Master’s degree under Ministerial Decree 270/2004
  • A Master’s degree under Ministerial Decree 509/1999
  • A pre-509/1999 Italian degree lasting at least four years
  • A second-level AFAM academic degree
  • An equivalent foreign academic qualification

Applicants Completing Degrees Later

Students who will complete their degrees before the programme start date may also apply.

However:

  • They must submit proof of graduation within 10 days after obtaining the qualification
  • Failure to submit documentation leads to exclusion

Admission Process

Admission to the doctoral programmes involves a competitive selection procedure.

Depending on the programme, the selection may include:

  1. Evaluation of academic qualifications
  2. Assessment of research proposals
  3. Written examinations
  4. Oral interviews
  5. Preliminary screening
  6. Language proficiency assessments

Interview Options

For programmes allowing online interviews:

  • Candidates may participate by videoconference
  • This option must be selected during application

The interview may also assess:

  • English proficiency
  • Other foreign language skills
  • Italian language knowledge for foreign applicants

Application Process

Applications must be submitted exclusively through the online platform.

Official application portal:

University of Florence PhD Application Portal

Required Documents

Applicants must upload:

For Italian Degrees

  • Identity document
  • Self-declaration of academic records
  • Thesis title and grades

For Foreign Degrees

  • Bachelor’s and Master’s degree copies
  • Academic transcripts
  • Grading scale
  • Thesis title and graduation score
  • Certified translations into English or Italian

Additional documents may be required depending on the doctoral programme.

Application Fee

Applicants must pay:

  • €30 non-refundable application fee

A separate fee is required for each doctoral programme application.

Important Application Deadline

Deadline:

  • June 11, 2026 at 12:00 noon (Italian time)

The university strongly advises applicants to submit applications well in advance because the online system automatically closes at the deadline.

Important Dates

Application Opening

  • May 12, 2026

Ranking Publication

  • By July 31, 2026

Enrolment Deadline for Successful Candidates

  • August 25, 2026

Programme Start Date

  • November 1, 2026

International Student Information

International students from non-EU countries must:

  • Register through the Universitaly portal
  • Apply for an Italian study visa
  • Provide translated and legalized academic documents

Accepted documentation may include:

  • Declaration of Value
  • CIMEA Statements of Comparability and Verification
  • Diploma Supplement for EU degrees

Attendance Requirements

PhD students must:

  • Attend full-time
  • Conduct continuous research activities
  • Participate in academic training
  • Complete international mobility requirements
  • Earn complementary skills credits

Intellectual Property and Research Publications

The University of Florence protects:

  • Intellectual property rights
  • Industrial property rights
  • Research publication rights

Doctoral students may also be required to sign confidentiality agreements regarding research and institutional information.

Why You Should Apply

The University of Florence PhD Programmes provide a remarkable opportunity to:

  • Study in Italy
  • Conduct advanced international research
  • Receive generous financial support
  • Join a multicultural academic environment
  • Build international research networks
  • Access European academic opportunities
  • Advance careers in academia, research, and innovation

For students seeking a prestigious European doctoral experience, this programme offers excellent academic, professional, and financial advantages.

Official Call and Information

Applicants can access the official programme details through the University of Florence website:

University of Florence Official PhD Call Page

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post University of Florence PhD Programmes 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunities with Scholarships, International Support, and Research Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026 in New York: Monthly Allowance Opportunity for EU Students Interested in Diplomacy and International Affairs
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Permanent Mission of Estonia to the United Nations has officially announced its Internship Fall 2026 opportunity in New York, offering students and young professionals an exceptional chance to gain practical diplomatic experience at the heart of global policymaking. This prestigious internship program allows selected candidates to work directly with Estonian diplomats at the United […]

The post Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026 in New York: Monthly Allowance Opportunity for EU Students Interested in Diplomacy and International Affairs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Permanent Mission of Estonia to the United Nations has officially announced its Internship Fall 2026 opportunity in New York, offering students and young professionals an exceptional chance to gain practical diplomatic experience at the heart of global policymaking. This prestigious internship program allows selected candidates to work directly with Estonian diplomats at the United Nations headquarters while contributing to important international discussions on climate policy, human rights, peace and security, humanitarian affairs, development, and institutional reforms.

For students passionate about international relations, diplomacy, global governance, public policy, law, development cooperation, or multilateral negotiations, this internship provides valuable exposure to the daily operations of a Permanent Mission to the United Nations.

The internship is based in New York City, operates full-time at 40 hours per week, and includes a monthly allowance to support interns during their placement.

Why the Estonia in UN Internship 2026 Is a Unique Opportunity

Interning at the Permanent Mission of Estonia to the United Nations offers participants direct exposure to international diplomacy and multilateral cooperation. Interns become part of a professional diplomatic environment where they support Estonia’s engagement with the UN system and contribute to discussions on major global issues affecting countries worldwide.

This internship is particularly valuable for students aiming to build careers in:

  • International relations
  • Diplomacy
  • Public policy
  • Human rights advocacy
  • Peace and security
  • International development
  • International law
  • Climate governance
  • Humanitarian affairs
  • Global governance institutions

Interns will work alongside experienced diplomats and policy experts while developing research, analytical, negotiation-monitoring, and communication skills that are highly valued in international careers.

About the Permanent Mission of Estonia to the United Nations

The Permanent Mission of Estonia to the UN represents Estonia’s interests within the United Nations system. The Mission actively participates in UN negotiations, policy discussions, international cooperation initiatives, and multilateral decision-making processes.

Located in New York City, the Mission engages in global discussions on:

  • Peace and international security
  • Human rights
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate change
  • Humanitarian response
  • Legal affairs
  • UN reforms and administration

The internship program allows participants to observe and contribute to these high-level international processes firsthand.

Internship Responsibilities

Selected interns will support the Mission’s delegates and diplomatic staff in several important areas. Interns will actively participate in the Mission’s daily work and gain real-world diplomatic experience.

Responsibilities include:

1. Supporting UN Meetings and Negotiations

Interns will assist Mission delegates in preparing for and following important UN meetings, negotiations, and policy discussions.

Tasks may include:

  • Preparing meeting briefs
  • Attending UN sessions
  • Taking notes during negotiations
  • Summarizing key developments
  • Assisting with follow-up documentation

2. Monitoring UN Proceedings

Interns will monitor discussions and developments within the UN system related to Estonia’s foreign policy priorities and areas of interest.

This may involve:

  • Tracking resolutions
  • Following committee discussions
  • Reporting policy developments
  • Monitoring voting outcomes
  • Identifying emerging diplomatic issues

3. Conducting Policy Research

Research is a major component of the internship. Interns will help diplomats by preparing background analyses and policy documents.

Research assignments may include:

  • Comparative policy analysis
  • International legal research
  • Human rights monitoring
  • Climate and development policy reviews
  • Geopolitical analysis

4. Drafting Briefing Notes and Reports

Interns will contribute to the drafting of:

  • Briefing notes
  • Policy memos
  • Analytical reports
  • Meeting summaries
  • Diplomatic background papers

This helps interns strengthen their professional writing and policy communication skills.

5. Working Across Specialized Policy Areas

Interns may contribute to one or several policy portfolios depending on their academic background, interests, and skills.

Available Policy Areas

Applicants are encouraged to indicate their preferred policy area(s) in their motivation letter.

The available areas include:

Climate Policy

Work on issues related to environmental governance, climate negotiations, sustainability, and global climate commitments.

Development Policy

Support initiatives focused on sustainable development, international cooperation, poverty reduction, and global development agendas.

Human Rights

Contribute to discussions and monitoring related to international human rights mechanisms and protections.

Humanitarian Affairs

Engage with global humanitarian response issues, emergency assistance, and crisis coordination.

Legal Affairs

Assist with legal analysis related to international law, treaties, UN resolutions, and diplomatic procedures.

Peace & Security

Follow discussions concerning conflict prevention, peacekeeping, international security, and geopolitical developments.

UN Administration & Reforms

Support work linked to institutional reform, governance modernization, and efficiency within the UN system.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026, applicants must meet several important requirements.

Candidates should:

  • Be enrolled in a bachelor’s or master’s degree program at a higher education institution
  • Demonstrate strong analytical and research skills
  • Show commitment to multilateral cooperation and international diplomacy
  • Possess creativity and problem-solving abilities
  • Have an open-minded, curious, and positive attitude
  • Be fluent in English
  • Have knowledge of another UN official language (considered an advantage)
  • Be eligible for and able to maintain security clearance by Estonian authorities

Important Language Advantage

Although English fluency is mandatory, applicants who speak additional UN official languages may have an advantage during the selection process.

Additional languages may include:

  • French
  • Spanish
  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • Russian

Internship Duration and Work Schedule

The internship generally lasts between 3 and 4 months.

Key details include:

  • Start period: September 2026 and throughout the year
  • Workload: 40 hours per week
  • Format: On-site in New York City
  • Number of positions: Multiple openings available

The exact internship dates will be agreed upon during the selection process.

Financial Information

The internship is technically unpaid; however, selected interns receive a monthly allowance.

Applicants should note that interns remain responsible for:

  • Accommodation costs
  • Daily living expenses
  • Travel costs
  • Health and personal insurance

The Permanent Mission of Estonia will assist selected candidates with the U.S. visa application process.

Benefits of the Estonia UN Internship

Participating in this internship offers numerous professional and academic benefits.

Key Benefits Include:

  1. Direct exposure to international diplomacy
  2. Hands-on experience within the United Nations system
  3. Opportunity to work with experienced diplomats
  4. Practical policy research experience
  5. Networking opportunities in global affairs
  6. Development of analytical and communication skills
  7. Exposure to high-level international negotiations
  8. Experience living and working in New York City
  9. Monthly financial allowance
  10. Enhanced career prospects in international organizations

How to Apply

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, meaning interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Applicants must send:

  • A detailed CV
  • A motivation letter

The motivation letter should clearly indicate:

  • Preferred policy area(s)
  • Availability period
  • Relevant academic or professional interests

Applications should be submitted via email to:

mission.newyork@mfa.ee

Application Deadline

There is no fixed deadline mentioned for this opportunity because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Early application is strongly recommended.

Official Opportunity Link

For more details about the internship opportunity, applicants can visit the official Estonia in UN page:

Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026 Official Page

Final Thoughts

The Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for EU students seeking real-world diplomatic experience inside one of the world’s most influential international institutions. From policy analysis and multilateral negotiations to global governance and peacebuilding, interns will gain meaningful exposure to the inner workings of international diplomacy.

For ambitious students interested in shaping global policy and pursuing careers within the United Nations, international organizations, foreign ministries, NGOs, or development institutions, this internship can serve as a strong foundation for future success.

 

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis. Send your CV and motivation letter, including your preferred policy area(s) and availability to: mission.newyork@mfa.ee

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship  opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Estonia in UN Internship Fall 2026 in New York: Monthly Allowance Opportunity for EU Students Interested in Diplomacy and International Affairs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNDP Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme 2026: Global Call for Climate Experts, Entrepreneurs, Investors, and Professionals to Mentor Youth-Led Climate Innovators
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), through the Youth4Climate initiative, has officially launched a global call for mentors for the Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme 2026. This international opportunity is inviting experienced professionals from across the world to support youth-led climate innovators through mentorship, technical guidance, masterclasses, ecosystem connections, and scale-up support. The mentorship programme is designed […]

The post UNDP Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme 2026: Global Call for Climate Experts, Entrepreneurs, Investors, and Professionals to Mentor Youth-Led Climate Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), through the Youth4Climate initiative, has officially launched a global call for mentors for the Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme 2026. This international opportunity is inviting experienced professionals from across the world to support youth-led climate innovators through mentorship, technical guidance, masterclasses, ecosystem connections, and scale-up support.

The mentorship programme is designed to connect young climate leaders with experienced professionals who can provide practical expertise, strategic insights, and real-world guidance to help youth-led climate projects grow sustainably and achieve long-term impact.

Selected mentors will participate in a six-month engagement running from June 2026 to December 2026 and will become part of a global ecosystem focused on accelerating youth-driven climate action, innovation, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development.

Professionals with expertise in climate finance, entrepreneurship, clean energy, sustainability, digital innovation, fundraising, impact measurement, communications, and business development are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline of May 18, 2026 at 23:59 CEST.

About Youth4Climate

Youth4Climate is a global initiative created to support and strengthen youth-led climate action around the world. The programme provides young climate innovators with:

  • Funding opportunities
  • Mentorship
  • Learning resources
  • Visibility
  • Networking opportunities
  • Ecosystem support
  • Capacity development

The initiative aims to empower young people who are building innovative climate solutions capable of addressing some of the world’s most urgent environmental and sustainability challenges.

Between 2023 and 2025, Youth4Climate supported numerous youth-led projects focused on:

  • Renewable energy
  • Climate-smart agriculture
  • Circular economy solutions
  • Waste management
  • Sustainable food systems
  • Digital climate innovation
  • Nature-based solutions

The programme recognizes that funding alone is not enough to ensure long-term impact. As a result, the mentorship programme was developed to ensure awardees gain access to experienced professionals, strategic guidance, technical support, and networks that can help them scale their initiatives effectively.

What is the Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme?

The Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme is a global mentorship initiative that pairs experienced professionals with Youth4Climate awardees and climate innovators.

The programme focuses on helping youth-led projects strengthen:

  • Project implementation
  • Operational systems
  • Strategic planning
  • Investment readiness
  • Business development
  • Sustainability pathways
  • Growth and scale-up potential

Mentors will support young innovators working across several climate and sustainability sectors and thematic areas.

Examples of Youth-Led Climate Solutions Supported

Youth4Climate awardees are developing innovative climate solutions across multiple sectors and regions globally.

Examples include:

  • Solar-powered cold storage systems for smallholder farmers
  • Digital platforms supporting climate-resilient agriculture
  • Clean energy solutions for underserved communities
  • Circular economy projects transforming waste into useful products
  • Climate-smart entrepreneurship initiatives
  • Sustainability and green innovation startups

The mentorship programme aims to help these projects overcome operational, technical, financial, and strategic challenges.

Duration of the Mentorship Programme

Selected mentors will participate in:

  • A 6-month engagement period
  • Running from June 2026 to December 2026

The programme is designed to offer flexibility while ensuring meaningful engagement between mentors and youth innovators.

Core Mentorship Responsibilities

The central component of the programme is one-to-one mentorship.

Mandatory Commitment

Selected mentors are expected to:

  • Commit a minimum of 2 hours per month
  • Schedule mentorship sessions flexibly based on availability
  • Provide tailored support and practical guidance
  • Help mentees navigate implementation and growth challenges

The mentorship arrangement is intentionally flexible to accommodate professionals from different industries and time zones.

Additional Ways Mentors Can Contribute

Beyond one-to-one mentorship, mentors may also participate in several additional programme activities depending on their expertise and availability.

1. Masterclasses

Mentors may lead group learning sessions covering topics such as:

  • Climate innovation
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Communications
  • Operational readiness
  • Business growth
  • Fundraising
  • Sustainability strategies

These sessions are designed to help awardees gain practical knowledge and industry insights.

2. Theory in Practice Talks

Experienced professionals will share:

  • Real-world experiences
  • Lessons learned
  • Growth journeys
  • Scaling strategies
  • Practical industry insights

The goal is to expose youth innovators to practical examples from successful climate and development practitioners.

3. Investor Showcases and Scaling Pathways

Mentors may also support advanced projects by helping them:

  • Improve business models
  • Strengthen investor pitches
  • Increase investment readiness
  • Develop scaling strategies
  • Build strategic partnerships

This component is particularly valuable for youth-led startups and scalable climate enterprises.

Why Professionals Should Join the Youth4Climate Mentorship Programme

The programme offers mentors the opportunity to contribute meaningfully to global climate action while expanding their professional networks and visibility.

Key Benefits of Becoming a Mentor

1. Support Real Climate Solutions

Mentors will directly contribute to projects addressing:

  • Climate resilience
  • Renewable energy
  • Food security
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Circular economy innovation
  • Community sustainability

2. Join a Global Network

Participants will connect with:

  • Climate experts
  • UNDP stakeholders
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Investors
  • Researchers
  • Innovation leaders
  • Sustainability practitioners

3. Gain Visibility and Recognition

Mentors will gain visibility through participation in a globally recognized UNDP-backed initiative supporting youth innovation and climate action.

4. Contribute to Youth Empowerment

The programme allows professionals to directly support the next generation of climate leaders and changemakers.

5. Flexible Engagement Structure

The mentorship structure is designed to accommodate busy professionals with:

  • Flexible scheduling
  • Limited monthly time requirements
  • Multiple engagement pathways

Who Can Apply?

UNDP is seeking experienced professionals from diverse sectors and industries.

Eligible Professionals Include:

  • Climate experts
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Corporate leaders
  • Investors
  • Academics
  • Researchers
  • Development professionals
  • Sustainability practitioners
  • Technical experts
  • Innovation specialists

Required Experience

Applicants must have:

  • At least 5 years of relevant work experience
  • Expertise in one or more relevant climate or development fields

Prior mentorship experience is welcomed but not mandatory.

Areas of Expertise Sought

Youth4Climate is particularly seeking mentors with expertise in:

Climate Finance and Fundraising

Professionals experienced in:

  • Grant acquisition
  • Investment mobilization
  • Climate finance mechanisms
  • Donor engagement

Investment Readiness and Pitching

Experts capable of helping startups and projects:

  • Develop investor presentations
  • Improve business cases
  • Attract funding

Startup Scaling and Business Development

Professionals with experience in:

  • Startup growth
  • Scaling innovation
  • Business strategy
  • Entrepreneurship ecosystems

Storytelling, Communications, and Advocacy

Mentors with expertise in:

  • Public relations
  • Branding
  • Strategic communications
  • Climate advocacy

Technical Climate and Sustainability Expertise

Including:

  • Sustainable energy
  • Food systems
  • Nature-based solutions
  • Digital innovation
